arts.ttl: Arsenal 'have gone BACKWARDS' as an attacking team and 'have not been enjoyable to watch,' Jamie Redknapp claims - as Gunners continue to chase Liverpool in Premier League title race
arts.sum: The Gunners are chasing Liverpool in the Premier League title race, currently six points behind the Reds, who have a game in hand, after both sides drew their respective games at the weekend.

arts.ttl: Man City ready to replace Kevin De Bruyne with €60m Arsenal target in 2025
arts.sum: The future of Kevin De Bruyne at Man City remains uncertain as the summer transfer window gets closer and the Premier League champions may already have a replacement lined up. The Belgian star has spent the last 10 years with the Manchester club but is out of contract at the Etihad Stadium at the end […] The post Man City ready to replace Kevin De Bruyne with €60m Arsenal target in 2025 appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Matheus Cunha avoided extended Premier League ban for two reasons amid Arsenal transfer chase
arts.sum: Arsenal transfer target Matheus Cunha avoided a longer suspension for his involvement in the unsavoury scenes after Wolves vs Ipswich Town due to two kind reasons, it has been confirmed. In the time it took to determine the punishment Cunha played three matches - scoring vs Leicester then doing so again alongside an assist against Man United.

arts.ttl: Arsenal v Newcastle United: Carabao Cup semi-final, first leg – live
arts.sum: Carabao Cup semi-final first leg news, 8pm GMT kick-off Rosicky leads contenders for Arsenal role | Email Scott Eddie Howe, who is taking charge of Newcastle for the 150th time tonight, speaks to Sky Sports. “Joe Willock’s running capacity is his biggest strength … a brilliant athlete who will help us in and out of possession … he’s good technically as well … he’ll add a different dimension to our midfield … we can’t play the same [without Bruno Guimaraes] … a tactical tweak … only Bruno can do what Bruno can do … we have to find a different way … it’s whether the team can adjust but I think we can.” VAR hasn’t been used in any of the previous rounds, but it’s back for the semi-finals and final, baby! Did you miss it? No, us neither. There’s a fancy new feature being rolled out tonight, too: should VAR have to intervene at any point, the on-field referee will announce and explain the decision to the crowd, over the mic, NFL-style. “We feel it will add some value,” insists PGMOL chief Howard Webb, which may or may not indicate how this caper is going to pan out, depending on your point of view / level of cynicism / recall of previous decisions, explanations, big ideas, etc. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: VAR took SEVEN seconds to agree Brighton pen vs Arsenal | Webb: Right call
arts.sum: VAR took just seven seconds to determine Brighton's controversial penalty against Arsenal was correct to be given, with PGMOL chief Howard Webb arguing it was a valid decision.

arts.ttl: Liverpool vs. Manchester United: Premier League title race blow for Arne Slot as Ruben Amorim's side give Arsenal boost
arts.sum: Liverpool's performance against Manchester United in the Premier League on Sunday is a cause for concern for boss Arne Slot, as the Dutchman looks to fend off Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Arsenal star Gabriel Jesus opens up on confidence issues before stunning return to form
arts.sum: Gabriel Jesus has returned to fine form for Arsenal and scored six goals in his last five games to provide the Gunners with a spark as they continue their Premier League challenge

arts.ttl: Major VAR change in force for Arsenal v Newcastle United's Carabao Cup semi-final
arts.sum: The move will be tested in both Carabao Cup semi-finals this week

arts.ttl: Exclusive: Fulham leading the race to sign £30m Brazilian striker this month with Arsenal also keen
Why isn’t Bukayo Saka playing for Arsenal against Newcastle in the Carabao Cup today?
arts.sum: Arsenal are looking to win their first major silverware since the 2019/20 FA Cup this season and have given themselves a more than realistic opportunity to lift the Carabao Cup (also known as the EFL Cup or the League Cup), in which they face Newcastle United in the first leg of the semifinals on Tuesday (January 7). Saka was forced off midway through the first half of the 5-1 Premier League win over Crystal Palace on December 21, with The Gunners later revealing the winger had suffered a serious hamstring injury .
